titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Victoria Sackville-West Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday January 23, 2021 at 04:08

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. Correspondence, 1889-1984. II. Legal papers, 1865-1936. III. Personal papers, 1872-1952. IV. Photographics, artwork, and clippings, 1908-1970.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Victoria Sackville-West Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Joan Winterkorn on the James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection Fund, 2017.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Victoria Sackville-West Papers. James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of correspondence, legal papers, personal papers, photographs, and artwork by or relating to Victoria Sackville-West, Lady Sackville, her daughter Vita Sackville-West, and other members of the Sackville-West family. Correspondents include Victoria's husband Lionel Sackville-West, Edwin Lutyens, Vita Sackville-West, Harold Nicolson, and Edward VII. The collection also includes third-party letters to Vita Sackville-West, Harold Nicolson, and Nigel Nicolson. The bulk of the legal papers relate to a trial concerning the Sackville-West inheritance and Knole House, the family home. Artwork includes sketches by Edwin Lutyens, Harold Nicolson, and Nigel Nicolson.

bioghist.head: Victoria Sackville-West
bioghist.p: Victoria Sackville-West, Lady Sackville, (1862-1937), was the daughter of Lionel Sackville-West, 2nd Baron Sackville and a Spanish dancer known as "Pepita de Oliva." Victoria married her first cousin, Lionel Edward Sackville-West, 3rd Baron Sackville, in 1892. Their daughter was the poet, novelist, and gardener Vita Sackville-West, who was married to British politician and writer Harold Nicolson.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Vita Sackville-West Papers (GEN MSS 925), Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.
Harold Nicolson Papers (GEN MSS 614), Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

persname.part: Edward VII, King of Great Britain, 1841-1910

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence includes letters to Lady Sackville from family members, William Astor, Edwin Lutyens, and others; letters from Lady Sackville to Vita Sackville-West, letters from others to Vita Sackville-West, and letters to Harold Nicolson and Nigel Nicolson.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Correspondence is organized into four subseries: Letters to Lady Sackville, 1889-1935; Lady Sackville letters to Vita Sackville-West, 1914-1931; Letters to Vita Sackville-West, circa 1911-1937; and Other correspondence, 1912-1984.

did.unittitle: Lady Sackville letters to Vita Sackville-West
Lady Sackville letters to Harold Nicolson

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Inheritance trial papers, 1865-1936; and Other legal papers, 1931-1933.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, court papers, and newspaper clippings relating to the Sackville-West inheritance trial and other lawsuits.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Correspondence, 1880-1936; Court papers, 1879-1909, undated; and Other inheritance trial papers, 1865-1910.

did.unittitle: General, A-Z, regarding Lionel Sackville-West's death
Baptism certificate of Ida Margaret Page
List of Knole jewels prepared by Amalia Sackville-West
Harry Wynne Nicholl vs Victoria Sackville-West, deposition

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Personal papers include Lady Sackville's diary, papers related to the Murchison letter, wills, a list of wedding presents, a deed, and other personal papers.

did.unittitle: Lady Sackville's diary and related notes
List of Victoria and Lionel Sackville-West's wedding presents
Inventory for sale of White Lodge contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of photographs of Lady Sackville, Lord Sackville in Egypt, and others; sketches by Edwin Lutyens, Harold Nicolson, and Nigel Nicolson; and clippings related to Lady Sackville, Lord Sackville, and Edwin Lutyens.

did.unittitle: Photographic prints of Lord Sackville in Egypt
Photographic prints of Lady Sackville, child
Newspaper clippings relating to Lord Sackville
Newspaper clippings, miscellany, related to Edwin Lutyens
